Maintaining a high standard of patient service. Why is knee replacement? Knee replacement surgery involves replacing damaged, worn or diseased cartilage and bone in your knee joint with an artificial joint. What we need surgery for knee? Knee replacement surgery (also known as arthroplasty) may be offered to you when your knee joint is worn or damaged. What does the procedure involve? Knee replacement surgery is usually performed either under general anaesthetic or under spinal or epidural anaesthetic. Benefits of knee replacement? The main benefits of knee replacement surgery are reduced or no knee pain and being able to move around so that you can undertake normal daily activities, participate in exercise and society. How long knee replacement last? For 80–90% of people who have total knee replacement, the new joint should last for 20 years or longer. Success rates Total knee replacement is one of the most successful procedures in all of medicine. In the vast majority of cases, it enables people to live richer, more active lives free of chronic knee pain. Knee Pain Shoulder Pain Pain in Hip Foot & Ankle Knee Pain A common complaint amongst nearly everyone is knee pain. There can be several reasons or causes for knee pain, such as a tear in the cartilage or ligament or the onset and effects of arthritis. Depending on the cause of your knee pain, treatments for knee pain can vary, from conservative nonsurgical care to minimally invasive knee surgery and knee replacements. Knee Arthroscopy ACL Reconstruction Osteoarthritis of the Knee Knee Replacement Recurrent Patellar Dislocation Osteotomy for Osteoarthritis of the Knee View More Details Shoulder Pain The wide range and versatile movement of the shoulder joint is also a source of instability and poses a risk for injury in patients. Shoulder pain can occur as a result of an injury or overuse, gradually causing you pain and discomfort over time or as a result of a rotator cuff injury. Treatments for shoulder pain can vary, from conservative nonsurgical procedures to minimally invasive surgery performed by a shoulder specialist. Frozen Shoulder Rotator Cuff Tears - Management Shoulder Arthroscopy Shoulder Dislocation and Recurrent Instability View More Details Pain in Hip Many people experience hip pain at some point in their lives. Hip pain can occur due to a variety of causes, and it is important to get to the bottom of your pain in order to receive the most effective hip pain treatment. If you've experienced hip joint pain for more than a week, you should seek treatment from a qualified hip specialist Hip Arthritis Hip Arthroscopy Total Hip Replacement View More Details Foot and Ankle Finding the cause of foot and ankle pain can be a difficult task. The foot has 26 bones and more than 30 joints. Tough bands of tissue called ligaments hold these together. The muscles, tendons, and ligaments work together with the many joints of the foot to control motion.
